tcs.title: CState C6 residency counters and CStates residency KPI
Multiple B2B cstates on same core

desc:
1. Architecture / Micro-architecture and Functionality

Feature Overview

TCD: CState C6 basic functionality
Test Plan: [NWP PM] C-State (PantherCove PNC)
Feature Area: [NWP PM] Core C1/C6 (Entry/Exit/Residency)
Active TCs: 14 | Rejected TCs: 0

Validation Scope

  • Validate CState C6 basic functionality functionality on NWP silicon
  • Part of [NWP PM] Core C1/C6 (Entry/Exit/Residency) feature area under [NWP PM] C-State (PantherCove PNC)
  • Verify hardware behavior matches NWP HAS/MAS specification
  • Confirm NWP-specific deltas from DMR baseline are correctly handled
2. Interfaces and Protocols
Refer to KB article and NWP HAS for register interface details.
KB: KB/pm_features/core_c_states/core_c_states_main.md
3. Reset, Power, and Clocking
  • Verify feature behavior is correctly restored after warm and cold reset
  • Confirm power domain and clock dependencies are respected
  • Validate BIOS initialization sequence programs feature correctly
⚠ Gap: Reset cross-product coverage should be verified in TC descriptions.
4. Programming Model
  • BIOS knob configuration as specified in TCD pre-conditions
  • OS interface via MSR/TPMI/sysfs as applicable to this feature
  • OOB PECI/Redfish path if feature has out-of-band configuration interface
5. Operational Behavior

Test Cases

HSDTitlePriorityStatus
22022423030CState Bios_knobs checkoutopen
22022423031CState Bios_knobs checkout PIVopen
22022423032CState C6 residency checkopen
22022423036CState C6 residency counters and CStates residency KPIopen
22022423038CState MSR Controlopen
22022423039CState dfx_ctrl_unprotected.core_cstate_limit checkout_siliconopen
22022423042Enable PEGA C-Statesopen
22022423044Multiple B2B cstates on same coreopen
22022423047[Solar] CStates - CStates_unsupported -- Exerciseopen
22022423050[Solar] CStates - CStates_unsupported_Random -- Exerciseopen
22022423053[Solar] CStates - CStates_unsupported_Random -- Verifyopen
22022423057[Solar]_CStates-CStates_unsupported--Verifyopen
16030768408CState C6 residency countersNoneopen
16030768409CState Fuse dfx_ctrl_unprotected.core_cstate_limit checkout_siliconNoneopen

Pass/Fail Criteria

  • PASS: Feature behaves per NWP HAS/MAS specification
  • PASS: No MCA or system hang during test execution
  • FAIL: Register values do not match expected per HAS
  • FAIL: Feature behavior differs from DMR baseline without documented NWP delta
6. Corner Cases & Error Handling
  • Boundary values: minimum and maximum register fields
  • Feature interaction with concurrent PM features
  • Error injection: invalid configuration should be rejected gracefully
⚠ Gap: Boundary and error injection scenarios should be detailed in individual TC descriptions.
7. Security / Safety / Policy
  • Fuse-gated disable: feature disabled via fuse should be reported correctly
  • Lock bit enforcement: verify write-locked registers cannot be overridden
  • OS vs BIOS vs OOB priority arbitration as per NWP HAS
⚠ Gap: Lock bit enforcement and policy arbitration should be validated in specific TCs.
8. References
TypeLinkScope
NWP HASNWP HAS PM FeaturesNWP PM feature list and deltas
NWP PM MASNWP IMH SoC PM MASNWP NIO PM MAS
KB ArticleKB/pm_features/core_c_states/core_c_states_main.mdFeature architecture, touchpoints, NWP delta

Pass/Fail Criteria

  • PASS: Feature behaves per NWP HAS/MAS specification
  • PASS: No MCA or system hang during test execution
  • FAIL: Register values do not match expected per HAS
  • FAIL: Feature behavior differs from DMR baseline without documented NWP delta
6. Corner Cases & Error Handling
  • Boundary values: minimum and maximum register fields
  • Feature interaction with concurrent PM features
  • Error injection: invalid configuration should be rejected gracefully
⚠ Gap: Boundary and error injection scenarios should be detailed in individual TC descriptions.
7. Security / Safety / Policy
  • Fuse-gated disable: feature disabled via fuse should be reported correctly
  • Lock bit enforcement: verify write-locked registers cannot be overridden
  • OS vs BIOS vs OOB priority arbitration as per NWP HAS
⚠ Gap: Lock bit enforcement and policy arbitration should be validated in specific TCs.
